scenarios.opening_query: I got hurt during my shift at the tribal casino and nobody can tell me whose rules apply.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > State || Workers' Comp > Other
scenarios.hidden_fact: The casino runs under the tribe's own workers' compensation ordinance, not the state system — sovereign rules, tribal forum, different deadlines.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the tribe's own comp ordinance — sovereign rules and a tribal forum, not the state system.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether tribal sovereignty displaces state comp
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I work for the state-licensed vendor inside the casino, not the tribe — ordinary state comp covers me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm with the state-licensed vendor inside, not the tribe — state comp covers me.

scenarios.opening_query: Twenty years of night-shift stress at the prison finally broke something in me, my doctor says.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > Stress Related || Labor & Employment > Public Employees - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The trigger for acting now is the fitness-for-duty exam they've ordered — my civil-service rights around that exam and any forced separation are the immediate fight, before the stress claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The trigger is the fitness-for-duty exam they ordered — my civil-service rights around it come before the stress claim.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a fitness-for-duty process is the live proceeding
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No exam ordered — the stress claim itself, with my doctor's causation letter, is what needs filing and defending.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No exam — the stress claim with my doctor's causation letter is the filing.

scenarios.opening_query: My National Guard weekend injury is being denied by everyone — civilian job, VA, all of them.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Drill-weekend injuries run through the military's line-of-duty and VA systems, not civilian comp — the LOD determination appeal is the actual case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Drill injuries run through line-of-duty and VA systems — the LOD determination appeal is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The injury actually happened Monday back at my warehouse job — the Guard connection is a red herring; state comp it is.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It actually happened Monday at the warehouse — state comp, the Guard part's a red herring.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer never reported my injury to anyone and now says it's too late for me to claim.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > State || Workers' Comp > Employers
scenarios.hidden_fact: Digging in, they carry NO comp insurance at all — the state's uninsured-employer process covers my claim while the state prosecutes them, a different track entirely.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They carry no comp insurance at all — the uninsured-employer process covers me while the state goes after them.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employer is uninsured triggering the special fund
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're insured — the 'too late' claim is just wrong, and the late-reporting penalty falls on them, not me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're insured — 'too late' is just wrong, and the reporting failure is their penalty.

scenarios.opening_query: Loading a barge on the river I slipped between the dock and the hull. I'm lucky to be writing this.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm permanently assigned to the barge crew and sleep aboard during runs — seaman status, Jones Act, not longshore.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm permanent barge crew and sleep aboard on runs — seaman status, Jones Act.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'm dockside labor — never crew, never sail; LHWA is my system.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Dockside labor — never crew. LHWA's my system.
scenarios.vetting_note: River variant of the seaman line.

scenarios.opening_query: My remote-work ergonomic injury claim got denied because it happened 'at home.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > State || Workers' Comp > Federal
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm a fully remote federal census bureau analyst — the claim belongs in the federal system, which has its own telework-injury rules the state examiner never applied.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm a remote federal census analyst — it's the federal system with its own telework rules, which the state examiner never applied.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether federal employment routes the telework claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I work for a private insurer from home — state comp, and the 'at home' denial ignores our signed telework agreement defining my workstation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm with a private insurer — state comp, and our signed telework agreement defines my workstation.
